sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section III, List of Answers on Terminology 75:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section III, List of Answers on Terminology 75:1
items.html: The name “world”‎ [world] begins primarily from Partzuf BON of AK, called the world of Nekudim. It is called by that name because ZA and Malchut of the inner vessels of phase four disappeared and became vessels for surrounding light, called garment and hall. Olam means He’elem [concealment], though at times the name is borrowed to refer to the Partzufim and Sefirot that preceded the world of Nekudim, but this is only a borrowed name. (Part 3, Chapter 8, item 1)
